questions.question: What does substance misuse mean?
options: A recognised medical treatment prescribed for pain
Needing more of a substance over time to feel the same effect
Any use of alcohol at all, even a single social drink
Using drugs or alcohol in a way that causes harm to a person's health, relationships, work or safety

questions.question: Which statement best describes dependence?
options: Choosing to drink only at social occasions
A sign that a person is weak and does not care about their health
A short-lived habit that always passes within a few days
The body and mind come to rely on the substance, so the person feels they need it and finds it very hard to stop

questions.question: What is meant by tolerance?
options: A physical or psychological need for a substance
Using a substance in a way that harms other people
Reducing the harm that substance use can cause
Needing more of a substance over time to feel the same effect

questions.question: Which of these is a reason a person might use drugs or alcohol, according to the course?
options: Because they want to be a burden on services
To cope with pain, trauma, grief, loneliness or stress
Because they are simply weak-willed
Because dependence is always a free lifestyle choice

questions.question: Which of these is a physical effect of long-term substance use described in the course?
options: Damage to the liver, heart and brain
A guaranteed cure for anxiety
Improved memory and sharper thinking
Stronger friendships and better finances

questions.question: Why is stigma harmful to people affected by drugs or alcohol?
options: It stops people asking for help and can affect the quality of their support
It is a necessary step before someone can get treatment
It has no real effect on people
It makes treatment work faster

questions.question: Which choice reflects the non-judgemental language the course encourages?
options: Describing someone as weak-willed
Describing someone as a lost cause
Describing someone as a person who uses drugs
Describing someone as an addict

questions.question: How much pure alcohol is in one unit?
options: Fifty millilitres
Ten millilitres
One hundred millilitres
One millilitre

questions.question: What is the UK low-risk guideline for regular drinking?
options: No more than fourteen units a week
No more than fourteen units a day
No more than forty units a week
There is no guideline at all

questions.question: If someone drinks up to the weekly guideline, how does the course advise they drink it?
options: Only at weekends in one sitting
Spread over three or more days with several drink-free days
All in a single session
It makes no difference how it is spread

questions.question: A person who is dependent on alcohol wants to cut down. What should you encourage?
options: Switching to spirits to make cutting down easier
Speaking to their GP or a specialist service rather than stopping abruptly
Ignoring it, as it is not your concern
Stopping suddenly and completely on their own straight away

questions.question: What do depressants such as alcohol do to the body?
options: Speed up the heart and raise blood pressure
Instantly reverse an overdose
Have no effect on breathing at all
Slow the brain and body down, including breathing

questions.question: Which describes the effect of stimulants such as cocaine?
options: They slow breathing until a person loses consciousness
They relieve pain and produce a deep sense of calm
They have no risks at all
They speed the body up, raising heart rate and blood pressure

questions.question: Why is an opioid overdose life-threatening?
options: Because opioids raise the heart rate too high
Because opioids make a person over-energetic
Because opioids have no effect on the body
Because opioids slow breathing

questions.question: Why is it useful to know the broad group a substance belongs to?
options: So you can provide the treatment yourself
So you can tell the person which drug to use instead
Because how a substance affects the body shapes the risks and the signs that someone may be unwell
So you can identify the exact drug yourself

questions.question: Which statement about prescription and over-the-counter medicines is correct?
options: Strong painkillers and sleeping tablets can lead to dependence
Dependence on a prescribed medicine is always the person's fault
Only illegal drugs are ever misused
They cannot cause dependence because they are legal

questions.question: You are worried a person is becoming dependent on a prescribed painkiller. What should you do?
options: Reduce their dose yourself to help them
Record what you have seen factually and report it to your senior or their GP
Add an extra painkiller to keep them comfortable
Withhold the medicine until they agree to stop

questions.question: Why is mixing two depressants, such as alcohol and opioids, especially dangerous?
options: Mixing them always reduces the risk of harm
They cancel each other out and are safe together
They speed the body up too much
They both slow breathing, which together can stop a person breathing altogether

questions.question: What does a harm-reduction approach mean?
options: Refusing to support anyone until they stop using completely
Reducing the harm that substance use can cause, even if the person is not ready to stop
Providing the treatment for dependence yourself
Ignoring the risks because the person has chosen to keep using

questions.question: A person you support is not eating, not heating their home and has unsecured medicines left out. What should you do?
options: Say nothing, as it is their own home
Tidy the medicines away and tell no one
Record what you have seen factually and report it as a safeguarding concern following your organisation's procedure
Tell the person off for not looking after themselves

questions.question: You are supporting an adult and become concerned that a child in the household is being neglected. What should you do?
options: Do nothing, as your role is only to support the adult
Report it without delay, following your organisation's child safeguarding procedure
Speak only to the parent and keep it between you
Wait several weeks to see whether things improve

questions.question: You find someone unconscious but breathing after suspected substance use. What should you do?
options: Try to make them sick to bring the substance back up
Call 999, place them in the recovery position and stay with them
Leave them to sleep it off and check back later
Give them a cold shower and strong coffee

questions.question: Which set of signs points to an opioid overdose?
options: High energy, fast speech and a racing heart
Improved mood and better coordination
Increased appetite and clearer thinking
Very slow or stopped breathing, pinpoint pupils and being hard to wake

questions.question: You have given naloxone to someone who has overdosed on opioids. What must you still do?
options: Nothing further, as naloxone is a permanent cure
Give a second substance to keep them awake
Leave them alone to recover
Still call 999, because naloxone is temporary and can wear off before the opioids do

questions.question: Which statement about being fit for work is correct?
options: Prescribed medicines never need to be mentioned to your manager
It is fine to work after drinking as long as you feel you are coping
Fitness for work only matters for nurses, not care workers
You must never care for people while under the influence of alcohol or drugs, and should tell your manager if a prescribed medicine could affect your work

questions.question: Dependence is recognised as a health condition, not a lack of willpower.
options: True
False

questions.question: Occasional social drinking is the same thing as dependence.
options: True
False

questions.question: According to the course, there is a completely safe level of drinking alcohol.
options: True
False

questions.question: Prescription and over-the-counter medicines can be misused and can lead to dependence, not just illegal drugs.
options: True
False

questions.question: If you find someone unconscious after substance use, you should try to make them sick to bring the substance back up.
options: True
False

questions.question: Self-neglect is recognised as a form of abuse and neglect under safeguarding guidance.
options: True
False

questions.question: It is acceptable to care for people after drinking, as long as you feel you are coping.
options: True
False

questions.question: Naloxone can temporarily reverse the effects of an opioid overdose by helping the person breathe again.
options: True
False

questions.question: Which of these are effects of substance use described in the course? (Select all that apply)
options: Permanent immunity to infection
Guaranteed improvement in physical health
Money problems and isolation
Low mood, anxiety and cravings
Damage to the liver, heart and brain

questions.question: Which of these are reasons a person might use drugs or alcohol, according to the course? (Select all that apply)
options: To cope with loneliness or stress
To cope with pain or trauma
Because they enjoy being judged
Because dependence proves they lack willpower
Alongside a mental health problem

questions.question: Which of these are signs of alcohol poisoning described in the course? (Select all that apply)
options: Vomiting
Pinpoint pupils
Cold or clammy skin
Confusion
A boost in energy and alertness

questions.question: Which of these are practical harm-reduction actions the course describes? (Select all that apply)
options: Encouraging someone to eat and stay hydrated
Encouraging someone to avoid using alone
Providing the treatment for dependence yourself
Refusing all support until they stop completely
Encouraging someone to keep to safer amounts

questions.question: You find someone unresponsive and barely breathing after suspected substance use. Which actions are correct? (Select all that apply)
options: Give them a cold shower to wake them
Leave them alone to sleep it off
Tell the operator what you have seen and, if you know, what they may have taken
Call 999 immediately
Stay with them and follow the operator's instructions

questions.question: Which of these can be symptoms of withdrawal, according to the course? (Select all that apply)
options: Sweating and shaking
Sickness and agitation
A guaranteed sense of calm and wellbeing
Anxiety and cravings
A permanent cure for dependence

questions.question: Which of these are correct responsibilities under your role and drug and alcohol policy? (Select all that apply)
options: Record concerns and escalate through the right channels
Adjust a person's prescribed medication yourself
Diagnose and treat substance misuse yourself
Never work while under the influence of alcohol or drugs
Tell your manager if a prescribed medicine could affect your work

questions.question: One unit of alcohol is ten ___ of pure alcohol.
The UK low-risk guideline is to drink no more than ___ units a week on a regular basis.
The medicine that can temporarily reverse an opioid overdose is ___.
If a person is unconscious but breathing, you should place them in the ___ position to help keep their airway clear.
When a person is not caring for their own health, hygiene or surroundings, this is called ___ and is a safeguarding concern.
